records.englishTranslation:
ABDULLAH GÜLER (Sivas) ON BEHALF OF THE AK PARTY GROUP - Dear President, Dear Vice President, Dear Minister.
(Exchange of words between AK PARTY and CHP ranks)
CHAIRMAN - Mr. Güler, wait a second. Mr. Güler, would you allow me?
Friends, the negotiations have gone very smoothly so far.
EYYÜP KADİR İNAN (İzmir) - Because they are disrespectful.
CHAIRMAN - I understand exchanging words, it has its limits, but no one has the right to make negotiations impossible here. Therefore.
(Noise and standing up from AK PARTY and CHP benches)
CHAIRMAN - Friends.
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Very shameful, very shameful; It's really a shame!
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Oh, take care of this Group Deputy Chairman. The Deputy Chairman of the Group who caused a scene is really shameful! The Group Deputy Chairman is a champion of causing trouble.
CHAIRMAN - Dear Deputy Chairman of the Group.
Mr. Guler, come on.
ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Dear President, our esteemed Vice President, our esteemed Minister, our esteemed Chairman of our political parties, our Group Chairmen, our Deputy Group Chairmen, our esteemed MPs; I have taken the floor on behalf of the AK PARTY Group to talk about the 2026 Central Government Budget Law Proposal and the 2024 Central Government Final Account Law Proposal, which are the 24th budget of the AK PARTY governments, the 8th budget of the Presidential government system, and the 3rd budget of the Turkey Century.
I would like to thank the esteemed Chairman and members of our Planning and Budget Commission, who worked day and night for forty days, our esteemed ministers, and the representatives of relevant public institutions for their retirement and contributions. I hope that the budget negotiations will be completed in a healthy and peaceful manner, and on this occasion, I greet the General Assembly and our beloved nation watching us on their screens with respect and love.
Before I begin my words, I wish God's mercy to our martyr police officer, Emre Albayrak, who was in charge of the Istanbul Police Department, who was martyred during an operation in Çekmeköy, Istanbul this morning. May he rest in peace, and we wish patience and condolences to his family and loved ones.
Dear President, dear members of parliament; We are going through a period in which global economic balances are being reshaped and political risks are deepening. There is a search for a new order in almost every field, from energy supply security to food supply, from financial markets to geopolitical balances. On the one hand, "It can't happen anymore." While the so-called conventional wars continue to increase, on the other hand, trade wars reverse all established assumptions. Many factors, from the uncertainties brought about by digital transformation to the climate crisis, from migration waves to increasing welfare inequalities, create an environment of unpredictability not only for countries but also on a global scale.
Unfortunately, it makes it very bad. We are in a world where the global growth rate has fallen below 3 percent and the debt burden exceeds 310 trillion dollars. In this very environment, Turkey, our country, and a country that is geopolitically in the middle of both hot wars and cyclical uncertainties, has managed to become one of the few countries that increase its production, protect its social expenditures, and grow without disrupting budget discipline.

It is difficult to achieve this, but we achieved it as a country. So, how did we do this? I have stated before that AK PARTY, which set out under the leadership of our President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, acted according to the vision of establishing the future, not to save the day, throughout its entire rule. Like the previous governments, they could have spent the day with policies to save the day and even chosen the easy option and undertaken many things that would lead our country into a pit of uncertainty, but we determined a new vision, put forward a new claim, we said: "We will establish a great and powerful Türkiye." We said: "We will make this century the Century of Türkiye." We challenged even though we knew it would cost. We acted knowing that every step we take to establish tomorrow's Turkey will also make today's Turkey stronger. Our 2023 vision, our 2053 vision and our 2071 vision are the exact equivalent of this claim.
All the budgets we have prepared as AK PARTY governments are, on the one hand, texts that determine the priorities of the fiscal policies we will implement, but at the same time, they are the numerical scales of our claim. Therefore, our 2026 budget, as the 8th budget of the Presidential government system, is extremely important as it is an indicator of the determination of our country, which is growing in stability and confidence and taking firm steps towards the goal of the Turkish Century. Our 2026 Central Government Budget Law Proposal, prepared with a citizen and service-oriented approach, is a stability and welfare budget.
We can define it as . The prepared budget proposal is based on the principle that each and every citizen of the society will benefit fairly from the opportunities provided by growth, without excluding any segment of the society. In the light of our development plan and medium-term program, strengthening the physical infrastructure, developing human capital and increasing productive capacity constitute the main axis of our budget proposal this year. The 2026 budget was prepared on the basis of an approach that protects the value of labor, observes social justice and supports all stakeholders of working life. Policies that support the full participation of women and youth in economic life form an integral part of our vision of inclusive growth.
Dear President, esteemed MPs; In the 2026 central government budget, we predict that budget expenses will be 18 trillion 929 billion liras and budget revenues will be 16 trillion 216 billion liras. In our 2026 budget, 3.6 trillion TL has been spent so far to reduce disaster risks, to quickly eliminate the damage caused by earthquakes, to meet the needs of our citizens living in the earthquake zone and to build earthquake-resistant cities, and this year we are allocating a total of 653 billion TL.
We have seen education as our top priority since 2002. We allocate a 15.3 percent share from the central government budget to education in 2026, which received only a 9.4 percent share in 2002. In this context, the budget of the Ministry of National Education will increase to 1 trillion 944 billion lira in 2026. When we include higher education, we have increased our education budget to 2 trillion 896 billion lira in 2026.

In order to ensure that our citizens benefit from health services in the best way, we are allocating 1 trillion 594 billion liras from the central government budget for health services in 2026. with the Ministry of Health and higher education institutions
Considering the health expenditures to be made in the Social Security Institution, we expect the total amount allocated to public resources in the field of health to reach 3 trillion 307 billion liras.
We are determined to spread the prosperity of our country to all layers of society with the principle of social state. As one of the countries with the best functioning and most comprehensive social support system in the world, we are increasing the social aid budget to 917 billion lira in 2026. This amount corresponds to 4.8 percent of our budget. We have increased the ratio of social aid and support to gross domestic product, which was 0.4 percent in 2002, to 1.2 percent in 2026.
While the agricultural product in our country was 36 billion 9 million lira in 2002, it increased to 2 trillion 428 billion lira in 2024. According to the World Bank Agricultural Product Data, our country ranked 4th in Europe behind Italy, France and Spain with 24.5 billion dollars in 2002, and rose to 1st place in 2024 with 74 billion dollars. In 2026, we allocate 888 billion lira from our budget to agriculture.
In 2026, an appropriation of 1 trillion 202 billion liras is expected to be used for defense expenditures and 953 billion for internal security, totaling 2 trillion 155 billion liras.
Mr. President, dear MPs; Türkiye continues its determination to increase its global competitiveness, and as a concrete indicator of this, our national income continues to increase. Our economic size has exceeded 1.3 trillion by 2024.
Mr. President, dear members of parliament; Türkiye is now exceeding its goals one by one and achieving significant successes, thanks to the vision put forward by our President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan and the strong solidarity of the People's Alliance. Türkiye, which received only 15 billion dollars of investment in the thirty years before 2003, has attracted approximately 282 billion dollars of direct investment to date during the AK Party period.
While only 5,600 companies with international capital were operating in our country in 2002, today we host more than 87 thousand multinational companies. Türkiye has now turned into an economic center where the production activities of these companies are supported by research and development centers, design teams, purchasing offices, logistics bases and regional production centers. Thanks to the policies implemented by our governments, Türkiye has now become a production base with more than 369 organized industrial zones and 52 industrial zones. Our President, Mr. Recep Tayyip Erdoğan's personal monitoring of the implementation of large-scale economic projects, making room for our business people on his foreign trips, inviting them and supporting international commercial connections have also strengthened our export performance.
With the export mobilization carried out, Türkiye has managed to exceed important export thresholds in a relatively short period of time.

Mr. President, dear members of parliament; I would like to tell you about the budget sizes, allocated shares and some compared figures of some developed and developing countries. Well, since 2020, we have experienced both the disruption of the supply chain and the pandemic process. Since then, I have been trying to explain to you the comparison between Germany, Japan, South Korea, Argentina and Türkiye. When we look at the change in budget sizes in US dollars between 2020 and 2026 in Türkiye and other countries, namely Germany, Japan, South Korea, Argentina and South Africa, we see that Türkiye has increased its budget size by 164.6 percent since 2020 and is ahead of these countries in terms of budget increase. Again, among the selected countries, the second country that allocated the most resources to education in the 2026 budget was Türkiye, with a share of 15.3 percent. Again, among the selected countries, Türkiye came 2nd after Germany and allocated 11.4% of its total budget to the defense industry. Among the selected countries, the country that allocated the most resources to agriculture was Türkiye, with 4.7 percent, 888 billion TL. A similar situation exists in many other titles. As can be seen, our budget is a budget prepared to make this century the Century of Türkiye, both in terms of its size and the share allocated to the areas.

SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Well, talk about your own budget.
ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - The average age of the bus fleet has increased to 12-14, the total IETT debt has increased to 34 billion TL, the estimated debt has increased to 50 billion TL, the ticket price has increased to 35 TL; How did this happen?
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Oh, what happened to this minimum wage earner, won't you ever tell me?

What happened to the civil servant, the retired, the unemployed? Will you never explain? What are you going to tell? What a shame, what a shame!
TAHSİN OCAKLI (Rize) - Tell us about the 2026 budget.
ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Look, friends, yes, the bankruptcy decision of Istanbul Metropolitan Municipality, İSFALT and BELTUR.
REŞAT KARAGÖZ (Amasya) - Mr. Recep, tell me how much raise he will give to minimum wage earners and retirees.
ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Requests a capital increase decision of 3 billion 500 million TL. Why? They wrote: "For other unforeseen reasons."
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (Istanbul) - Give, give, it is not so easy to slander; Give more time so that these slanders have time.
AHMET VEHBİ BAKIRLIOĞLU (Manisa) - He will talk about the budget, he will talk about retirement, give him more time; He will talk about the minimum wage earner, the civil servant, the farmer, he forgot them.
CHAIRMAN - Mr. Güler, please complete your words.
Here you go.
ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Dear friends, Mr. Özgür Özel gave a task to TOKİ.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Give it time because it needs to talk about retirees, it needs to talk about minimum wage earners.
ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Mr. Özgür Özel, when you went to Sındırgı, you gave TOKİ the task: "You will monitor the earthquake residences." saying. We are here for you too.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Citizens want to hear something from you about minimum wage and retirees.
ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - Oh,
Shut up brother! Gee!
(Applause from the AK PARTY benches, noise from the CHP benches)
CHAIRMAN - Dear friends, wait a minute. There is no such negotiation procedure, until the morning.
AHMET VEHBİ BAKIRLIOĞLU (Manisa) - Yes, tell him to address the General Assembly, is there such a thing! You haven't warned me for an hour!
CHAIRMAN - Could you please be silent? You just stay calm.
Dear friends, I request the AK PARTY Group and the CHP Group to remain calm.
I ask the esteemed speaker to address the General Assembly and begin a final minute.
Here you go please.
ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - I am not involved in the indictment. Those who are tried and convicted in Izmir will be punished, but there are 4 thousand people there who are victims. Look, you have locked in İzmir Bayraklı, Çiğli and also Uzundere.
ALİ MAHİR BAŞARIR (Mersin) - It is being done, it is being done.
MEHMET TAHTASIZ (Çorum) - 80 percent of Türkiye is hungry!
ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - These residences. The people you victimized are 4 thousand people. If you listen to these people, watch here instead of watching TOKİ, watch your municipalities. (Sounds of "Bravo" and applause from the AK PARTY benches; noises from the CHP benches)
İSMET GÜNEŞHAN (Çanakkale) - How much will the pensions be?
ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - They promised people "We will build public housing." There are locks on their doors. Look, our houses that were delivered in Izmir after the Bayraklı earthquake - I was in the earthquake research commission. Please relieve the suffering of nearly 4 thousand people.
İSMET GÜNEŞHAN (Çanakkale) - How much will the minimum wage be?
AHMET VEHBİ BAKIRLIOĞLU (Manisa) - You said you would give land to 500 thousand people. Where are the plots? You took their money, where are the lands? Does the state do this?

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - We have a lot more to tell, but I'm content with this.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - While respectfully greeting the Supreme General Assembly, I wish the 2026 budget to be beneficial and at the same time I would like to thank our friends who contributed.
My respects. (Sounds of "Bravo" and applause from the AK PARTY benches)

records.originalTurkish:
AK PARTİ GRUBU ADINA ABDULLAH GÜLER (Sivas) - Saygıdeğer Başkanım, çok saygıdeğer milletvekilleri; yüce Genel Kurulu saygıyla selamlıyorum.

Tabii, Özgür Bey çok heyecanlı yani o kadar heyecanlı ki zannedersiniz ki Meclis İçtüzüğü kapsamında sürekli çalışmayı arzu eden... 27'nci Yasama Döneminde 310 tane kanun çıkartmışız, bunların hepsinde bilfiil karar yeter sayısı istememiş, yoklamayla yeter sayısı istememiş, "Meclis sürekli çalışsın." demiş; salı, çarşamba, perşembe günleri Meclis bir şekilde yeter sayı olmadığı için, kapanması zaruret arz ettiği için İç Tüzük gereğince kapanmış.

ÖZGÜR ÖZEL (Manisa) - Kim yapmış onu?

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Kim yapmış?

ÖZGÜR ÖZEL (Manisa) - Gelseydiniz kapanmazdı.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Yoklamayla yeter sayısını kim istemiş?

BURCU KÖKSAL (Afyonkarahisar) - Siz yoktunuz ki.

ALİ MAHİR BAŞARIR (Mersin) - Gelmediniz, gelseydiniz.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Yoklamayla yeter sayısını kim istemiş?

BURCU KÖKSAL (Afyonkarahisar) - Meclise gelmediniz ki.

ALİ MAHİR BAŞARIR (Mersin) - Siz olmadığınız için kapandı.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Niye girmediniz?

BURCU KÖKSAL (Afyonkarahisar) - Meclise gelmediniz siz.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Yoklamayla yeter sayısı isteyip niye girmediniz?

ALİ MAHİR BAŞARIR (Mersin) - Siz olmadığınız için kapandı.

BURCU KÖKSAL (Afyonkarahisar) - Vekilleriniz gelmediği için kapandı.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Hani çok çalışma istiyorsunuz ya, yoklamayla yeter sayısı isteyenler yoklamaya girmiyor; Allah Allah!

ALİ MAHİR BAŞARIR (Mersin) - Siz olmadığınız için kapandı, siz.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Salı, çarşamba, perşembe Meclis çalışacak ama Cumhuriyet Halk Partisi keyfinden dolayı çalışmayacak.

YUNUS EMRE (İstanbul) - Zamları anlat sen!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Arkadaşlar, size rağmen 27'nci Yasama Döneminde aziz milletimizle ilgili 310 tane -uluslararası sözleşmeler dâhil- kanuni düzenlemeyi hayata geçirdik, size rağmen. (AK PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar)

YUNUS EMRE (İstanbul) - Zamları anlat, zamları!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Cumhur İttifakı'yla beraber, omuz omuza, size rağmen hayata geçirdik; bunun bilinmesini isterim. (CHP sıralarından gürültüler)

İki: Meclis İçtüzüğü 5'inci madde "1 Temmuzdan itibaren Meclis tatile girer." der.

MAHİR POLAT (İzmir) - Yüzde 82 zamlar ne?

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Peki, o tarih itibarıyla AK PARTİ Grubu bir önerge vermiş Meclis çalışmaya devam etsin diye, değil mi? Siz çalışma istiyordunuz, değil mi? Grup önerimize ne dediniz efendim, ne dediniz: "Hayır, biz çalışmak istemiyoruz." Reddettiniz değil mi? (AK PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar) Allah Allah!

ÖZGÜR ÖZEL (Manisa) - Hadi oradan!

ALİ MAHİR BAŞARIR (Mersin) - Yalan, yalan!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Ya, bir dakika. AK PARTİ Grubu diyor ki: "1 Temmuzdan sonra çalışmaya devam edelim."

GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (İstanbul) - Hadi buyurun, devam edelim.

ÖZGÜR ÖZEL (Manisa) - Hadi, hadi!

ALİ MAHİR BAŞARIR (Mersin) - Tamam, hadi o zaman; hadi, hadi o zaman.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Cumhuriyet Halk Partisi diyor ki: "Hayır." Allah Allah, niye acaba; niye acaba, çok merak ediyorum!

GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (İstanbul) - Hadi devam edelim.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Zam geldiği için devam diyoruz; fakir fukara zamdan ezildiği için devam diyoruz.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Seçimlerin bittiği günün ertesi günü AK PARTİ'de seçim çalışmaları başlar.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Zam için "Gelin çalışalım." diyoruz, zam için, zam.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Biz tatilde değiliz, biz seçim bölgesindeyiz. Arkadaşlarımız şu anda seçim bölgelerinde çalışmalar yapıyorlar, aziz milletimizi dinliyorlar, notlarını alıyorlar. İnşallah ekim ayıyla beraber aziz milletimize hizmete girecek ve onların her türlü beklentisini karşılayacak kanuni düzenlemeyi de hayata geçireceğiz; bunların da bilinmesini isterim.

ÖZGÜR KARABAT (Çanakkale) - Seçimi bırak, geçim çalışması yap.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Cumhurbaşkanı yeni paketinizde açıkladı, yeni bir külfet daha geldi. Neyin farkındasınız ya?

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Diğer bir husus, değerli arkadaşlar, Meclis İçtüzüğü'müzün 7'nci maddesi kapsamı içerisinde -Müsavat Bey de bahsetti- bir olağanüstü toplantı... Gerçi, İç Tüzük'ümüzde "olağanüstü toplantı" diye bir kavram yok ama olağanüstü birleşimle ilgili bir düzenleme var.

Peki, orada, 7'nci maddede gerekçesiyle beraber, burada olağanüstü toplantının milletvekillerinin asgari beşte 1'inin isteği üzerine toplanacağını bahsediyor.

GÖKAN ZEYBEK (İstanbul) - Zamlardan daha büyük ne gerekçe olabilir?

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Peki, bugüne kadar yani 82 Anayasası'ndan sonra Meclisimiz kaç kere olağanüstü toplantıya geçmiş?

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Hiç böyle zam gelmedi ki!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Bugünkü toplantıyla beraber 52 tane toplantı gerçekleştirmiştir. Bunun 6 tanesi...

HASAN UFUK ÇAKIR (Mersin) - Mazota gel, mazota! Mazota gel Beyefendi, mazota gel! Zamma gel, zamma! (AK PARTİ sıralarından gürültüler)

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Geleceğim.

HASAN UFUK ÇAKIR (Mersin) - Hadi gel bakalım!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Geleceğim, geleceğim. Biraz sonra geleceğim. Önce Özgür Bey'in dile getirdiği hususları dile getirelim, sonra geleceğiz; sırayla. O size göre değil Beyefendi, Sayın Milletvekili; sırayla; tamam. (AK PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar)

Peki, mevcut duruma göre, İç Tüzük kapsamı içerisinde gerekçesiyle beraber 120 milletvekili bunu talep etmiş ve Meclis Başkanlığımız da bugün itibarıyla olağanüstü toplantıya karar vermiş. Peki, olağanüstü toplantı kararı verilirken bugünkü 52 tane taleple beraber 6 tanesi cevapsız bırakılmış veya reddedilmiş, diğerlerine de farklı cevaplar verilmiş. Orada da gerekçelerine bakıldığında ne denilmiş, biliyor musunuz? Olağanüstü bir toplantı yapılarak genel görüşme açılmasını zorunlu kılacak nitelikte acil bir durumun olmadığı. "Olağanüstü" kavramını hukukçu arkadaşlarımız bilir, süreli ve acil bir durumun ortaya çıkmış olması. Bizim, bundan on gün önce, 15 Temmuzda, gece saat 01.00 gibi oylamasını yaptığımız bir kanuni düzenlememiz geçti ek bütçeyle beraber, yürütme, yürürlükle beraber yaklaşık 32 maddelik bir torba yasamız geçti. Bu kanunun, şu anda gerekçesi olan yani ÖTV, KDV artışlarıyla ilgili gerekçe olan hususların tamamını orada görüştük. Peki, görüşme safahatımız nedir? Toplamda, Genel Kurul aşamasında sekiz saat -merkezî bütçeyle ilgili, yönetim bütçesindeki cetvellerle ilgili- Komisyon aşamasında on bir saat yirmi dakika ve on yedi saat otuz beş dakika Meclis çalışma yapmış. Burada, bütün muhalefet partilerinin hatipleri her türlü düşüncelerini ileri sürmüş bu hususlarla ilgili, tamamı değerlendirilmiş.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - O kanundan sonra yüzde kaç zam geldi?

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Peki, tamamı değerlendirilen bu hususta hangi acil durum, hangi süreli bir iş gündeme geldi de bu olağanüstü toplantı ihtiyacı doğdu ve şu anda bu ihtiyaca karşı neler söylendi?

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Yüzde kaç zam geldi, yüzde kaç? Şanlıurfa'nın tüm elektrikleri kesik, suları kesik; daha nasıl olsun? (AK PARTİ sıralarından "Dinle, dinle!" sesleri)

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Arkadaşlar dinleyelim lütfen. Öncelikle bir cevapları vereyim.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Anladım da Beyefendi, olağanüstü gelişmeleri söylüyorum size. Yüzde kaç zam geldi? (AK PARTİ sıralarından gürültüler)

BAŞKAN - Bir dakika... Bir dakika...

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Sakin olalım arkadaşlar.

BAŞKAN - Abdullah Bey, bir dakika...

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Şanlıurfa'nın tüm elektrikleri kesik şu anda, 45 derece sıcaklıkta Urfa'nın elektrikleri kesik kardeşim.

BAŞKAN - Ya, Sayın Tanal...

Değerli milletvekilleri, biraz evvel Özgür Bey konuşurken bu gruptan bir arkadaşımız laf atıyordu, ben haber gönderdim, laf atmanın doğru olmadığını söyledim, bir daha laf atmadı.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Başkanım...

BAŞKAN - Bir dakika... Tanal, sen hep konuşuyorsun ama be kardeşim!

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Bakın, 14'ünde Meclis kapandı mı? Kapandı. 15'inde Cumhurbaşkanı kararnamesiyle zam gelmedi mi? Daha ne olsun? Nasıl bir olağanüstü...

BAŞKAN - Seni çağırıyorlar, Urfa'dan bir heyet gelmiş.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Anladım, 14'ünde Meclis kapandı, 15'inde de zam geldi.

BAŞKAN - Tanal, biraz susar mısın. Ama hep konuşuyorsun sen ya!

Buyurun.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Değerli arkadaşlar, olağanüstü toplantı davetinin de talebinin de Meclisimizin geçmişteki uygulamaları ışığında süreli, takvimli ve aciliyet gerektiren kullanımlarda ihtiyaç olduğu da ortadadır, lütfen, bu hususa da dikkatinizi çekiyorum. Çünkü geçen sene de 5 Ağustosta, aynı şekilde, yine Cumhuriyet Halk Partisinin milletvekilleri tarafından bir davet olmuştu ve toplantı yeter sayısı olmadığı için Meclis o gün toplanamamıştı. Sebep neydi? İçeriğiyle ilgili, diğer siyasi partilerle ilgili, aciliyet, süreli, günlü ve takvimli işlerle ilgili yeterli, inandırıcı gerekçe sunamadığı içindi.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Yani rahatınızı mı bozduk?

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Lütfen bu hassasiyetimizi de bilelim.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Anladım, yani rahatınızı mı bozduk? Millet için sizi çağırdık, çok zam geldiği için.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Meclisimiz, 27'nci Yasama Dönemi içerisinde 310 kanun düzenlemesi yapmış, on bin saatin üzerinde çalışma yapmış, 300 bin sayfadan ibaret de tutanak tutmuştur yani çalışkan, işinin ve gündeminin hâkimi bir Meclis vardır. Lütfen arkadaşlarımızı bu şekilde tatildeymiş gibi de itham etmeyin.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Ya, kardeşim, Meclis daha vatandaşlara oturacak oda bulamadı, oda! Oturacak yerleri yok daha, neye hâkim ya! Meclis neye hâkim, daha oturacak yerleri yok vekillerin!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Gerçi "tatil" deyince... "O kişi ki her işi kendinden bilir." diye bir atasözümüz vardır. Yani arkadaşlarımızın kendileri tatilde olabilir, ancak bizim milletvekillerinin hiçbirisi...

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Vekillerin oturacak odaları yok daha, neye hâkim ya! Oturacak odaları yok!

İBRAHİM EYYÜPOĞLU (Şanlıurfa) - Ya, dinlemesini bil!

ÖZLEM ZENGİN (İstanbul) - Lütfen ya, susunuz ya!

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Oturacak odaları yok! Kaç tane vekil boşta!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Arkadaşlar... Arkadaşlar...

SAYIN BAYAR ÖZSOY (Kayseri) - Ayıp be!

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Ayıp yaptığınız, vatandaş, fakir fukara!

SAYIN BAYAR ÖZSOY (Kayseri) - Senin yaptığın ayıp ya!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Değerli arkadaşlar... Değerli arkadaşlar...

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Zam getiriyorsun, zam! Ne ayıbı? Hırsızlık mı yaptık? Hırsızlık yapanlar ayıp yapıyor!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Değerli arkadaşlar, "tatil" deyince bizim milletimizin hafızasında bir şey var. Mesela, depremde tatile nasıl gidilebilir? Mesela, yangın ve sel felaketi olmuş, tatile nasıl gidilebilir? Mesela, kar yolları kapatmış, değil mi? Acil kamu hizmetine ihtiyaç var; tatile nasıl gidilebilir? (AK PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar) Mesela bunun cevabını milletimiz çok iyi biliyor ama ben çok iyi biliyorum ki bizim Cumhur İttifakı'yla beraber bütün milletvekillerimiz şu anda tatilde değiller, sahadalar; sosyal medya hesapları açık, kaynaktan da izlenebilir. (AK PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar) Ancak kimlerin tatilde olduğu, kimlerin yatlarda, katlarda gezdiği de belli. Ha, şunu da söylüyorum arkadaşlar: Milletvekili bir robot değildir, tatil de çok insani bir haktır. Yani Özgür Bey söyledi kapatırken "İki hafta, üç hafta, dört hafta... Herkesin ailesi var, çocukları var..."

ÖZGÜR ÖZEL (Manisa) - Bir iki hafta... Ben "Bir iki." dedim, makulü o.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Şimdi "Bir iki." kayıtlara bakın "İki üç hafta." diyorsunuz "Dört hafta." diyorsunuz; önemli değil.

Arkadaşlar, bakın, herkesin evlatları var, çocukları var, sınavlardan çıkmışlar, çok insani bir ihtiyaç bu; kanunlarda yer almış bir ihtiyacı sanki özel bir durummuş gibi anlatmanızı da ayıplıyorum burada. Lütfen, bu ifadelerinizi düzeltin. Milletvekillerimiz de seçim çevresinde, sadece burada, Genel Kurul çalışmalarına katılmıyorlar, sadece komisyonlara katılmıyorlar, Genel Kurulun dışında da seçim bölgelerinde, özellikle depremi yaşayan 11 tane ilimizin bütün milletvekilleri her gün orada. Çünkü çok ağır bir deprem geçirdik, ülkemize toplam maliyeti önümüzdeki dönemde 104 milyar doları bulacak büyük bir felaketi yaşadık. Bulgaristan büyüklüğündeki bir ülkeyi yıkıma uğratacak bir felaketi yaşadık, aynı o büyüklükte. Altyapıdan üstyapıya, orada istihdamın sağlanması, yeniden sanayi bölgelerinin kurulması ki ben teşekkür ediyorum bütün milletvekillerine. Geçen çıkardığımız torba yasa içerisinde hem AFAD'la ilgili hak sahipliği için hem de yine, Sanayi ve Teknoloji Bakanlığımızın yeni sanayi bölgelerinin kurmasıyla ilgili birçok düzenlemeyi hayata geçirdik; bunların şu anda arkadaşlarımız takipçisi, ben teşekkür ediyorum onlara. Dolayısıyla, bizim "tatil" diye bir derdimiz yok, aziz milletimizin hizmetinde her zaman da çalışmaya hazırız.

İkincisi, Özgür Bey cezaevinde bulunan bazı gazetecilerden bahsetti. Bağımsız yargı, kendi tarafsız ve bağımsız hâliyle yargılamaları devam ettiriyor.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Bugün gazeteciler gözaltına alındı, bugün, bugün.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Ya, tamam da bu cezaevinde olan basın mensuplarını düşünürken şunu niye düşünmüyoruz acaba: Şu belediyelerden, partilerden maaşa bağlanan gazeteciler, basın mensupları için, bunlarla ilgili bir açıklamanız olacak mı acaba, çok merak ediyorum. Hazin bir belge yayınlandı değil mi, bazı gazeteciler belgelendi; hazin bir belge, yıllık kaç milyon TL, bilmem ne televizyonuyla anlaşma yapıldığıyla alakalı bir belge yayınlandı. 24 Temmuz Gazeteciler ve Basın Bayramı Günü'nde buna karşı da bir cevap verilebilirdi herhâlde, değil mi? Basın mensuplarının cezaevinde olduğunu ifade eden bir düşünce herhâlde buna da bir cevap verirler diye düşünüyorum.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Bugün, bugün gazeteciler gözaltına alındı.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Değerli arkadaşlar, AK PARTİ'nin yirmi yıllık bir...

GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (İstanbul) - Anayasa Mahkemesi ve Sayıştay denetimden geçmiş bir protokolden bahsediyorsunuz.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Bu protokolü keşke yaparken de açıkça ifşa etseydiniz.

GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (İstanbul) - Anayasa Mahkemesi ve Sayıştay denetiminden geçmiş bir protokol...

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Noterden ihtarnameyle değil, herkes yaparken...

GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (İstanbul) - Biz yandaş medyadan bahsetmiyoruz.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Peki, bu belediyelerden de şu şeylerden maaş alanları da açıklarsanız memnun oluruz.

GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (İstanbul) - Hepsini açıklayalım, sorun, hepsini açıklayalım.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Devamla) - Değerli arkadaşlar, yirmi yıllık AK PARTİ iktidarı döneminde birçok kazanım sağladık. Biz, AK PARTİ olarak, 2024-2028 dönemi arasında yıllık ortalama yüzde 5,5 seviyesinde büyümeyi, 2028 yılı sonunda 1,5 trilyon dolar millî gelir büyüklüğüne ulaşmayı, 16 bin dolarlık kişi başı gelir düzeyine ulaşmayı, 2022 yılı sonu itibarıyla 32 milyon civarında olan toplam istihdamı 6 milyon artışla 2028 yılının sonunda 38 milyona yükseltmeyi, işsizlik oranının kademeli olarak gerilemesini sağlayarak işsizlik oranını yüzde 7'nin altına indirmeyi, 2022 yılı sonu itibarıyla 254,2 milyar dolar olan ihracatımızı 2028 yılında en az 400 milyar dolara yükseltmeyi, yeni dönemde ülkemizi ziyaret eden turist sayısını 90 milyona, toplam geliri de 100 milyar dolara çıkartmayı, enflasyonu tek haneli rakamlara indirmeyi, finansal enstrümanları daha da geliştirerek TL cinsi tasarrufları artırmayı hedefliyoruz. Para, maliye, gelirler ve finansal sektör politikalarında ise daha güçlü bir eş güdüm sağlamakta da kararlıyız. Mali disiplini ekonomimize güveni artıran bir çıpa olarak korumaya ve güçlendirmeye devam edeceğiz.

Sayın Başkan, değerli milletvekilleri; iktisat politikalarının ortak amacı gelir dağılımındaki dengeyi, fiyat istikrarını ve tam istihdamı gerçekleştirmektir. Hedeflenen politikaların başarıyla yürütülmesinde mali politikaları da kullanabilecek etkili araçlara sahibiz. Bunlardan en önemlisi vergidir. Vergi, gelir dağılımından mal ve hizmetlerin fiyatlarını belirlemeye kadar pek çok alanda etkili maliye politikası aracıdır. Bu sebeple, birçok ülkede vergiler ülke şartlarına göre çeşitlilik göstermektedir. Biraz önce de belirttiğimiz gibi, 104 milyar doları bulan, yüzyılın afeti olarak kabul edilen deprem, ülkemizin ekonomisine ve kamu maliyesine ciddi bir yük meydana getirmiştir. Bu sebeple, depremin oluşturduğu mali etkilerin bir kısmını telafi edebilmek adına vergi düzenlemelerinin yapılması zaruret oluşturmuştur. Depremin yol açtığı ilave maliyetleri bir miktar azaltma ve bütçe disiplinini yeniden tesis etmek amacına yönelik olarak alınan gelir tedbirlerimiz, ayrıca enflasyonla mücadelede para politikası ile maliye politikası arasındaki eş güdümü güçlendirmemize de katkıda bulunacaktır.

Sayın Başkan, değerli milletvekilleri; ülkelerin vergi yapılarını analiz etmede kullanılan önemli değişken ise toplam vergi hasılatının gayrisafi yurt içi hasıla içindeki payını gösteren vergi yükü kavramıdır. Bu oranın zaman içindeki değişimiyle birlikte vergi gelirlerinin kompozisyonu ve büyümenin sektörel değişimi, vergi yapısı ve büyümeye ilişkin önemli bilgiler içermektedir. Burada hemen belirtmek isterim ki Türkiye, OECD ülkeleri içinde vergi yükü en düşük ülkelerden biridir. Dolaylı ve dolaysız vergilerin bu kompozisyon içindeki oransal ağırlığı ise siyasi tercihe ve ülkenin ihtiyaçlarına göre değişkenlik göstermektedir. AK PARTİ olarak vergi yükünü ve kompozisyonunu önümüzdeki dönemde mali disiplinden asla taviz vermeden milletimizin menfaati ve ekonomik hedeflerimiz doğrultusunda belirleyeceğiz ve belirlemeye devam edeceğiz. Para politikasının yanı sıra maliye politikası, yapısal reformlar ile Türkiye'de finansal istikrarı pekiştirerek istikrar ve güven içinde büyümeyi hedeflemekteyiz. Türkiye Yüzyılı vizyonumuz doğrultusunda istihdamı artıracak, büyümenin nimetlerini her zaman olduğu gibi adaletli şekilde dağıtmaya devam edeceğiz. Türkiye'de yatırım ortamının iyileştirilmesi için millî değerlerimize uygun, doğru tanımlanmış ve sonuç odaklı reform adımlarımızı sürdürmeye devam edeceğiz. Orta vadeli programda makro göstergelerle kamu politikalarını güncelleyerek yapısal reformları ortaya koyacak ve kaynak tahsisini bu çerçevede yönlendirmiş olacağız. Şunun altını bir kez daha çizmek istiyoruz ki Cumhurbaşkanımız Sayın Recep Tayyip Erdoğan liderliğinde ülkemizi hedefleriyle buluşturma mücadelemizi hız kesmeden sürdürüyoruz ve sürdürmeye devam edeceğiz.
records.englishTranslation:
ABDULLAH GÜLER (Sivas) ON BEHALF OF THE AK PARTY GROUP - Dear President, very respected members of parliament; I respectfully salute the Supreme General Assembly.

Of course, Mr. Özgür is very excited, so excited that you would think that those who want to work continuously within the scope of the Internal Regulations of the Assembly... We passed 310 laws in the 27th Legislative Term, in all of them, they did not ask for a quorum by actual decision, they did not ask for a quorum by roll call, "Let the Parliament work continuously." he said; On Tuesdays, Wednesdays and Thursdays, the Assembly was closed in accordance with the Internal Regulations because there was not a quorum and it was necessary to close it.

ÖZGÜR ÖZEL (Manisa) - Who did that?

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - Who did it?

ÖZGÜR ÖZEL (Manisa) - If you had come, it would not have closed.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - Who asked for a quorum by roll call?

BURCU KÖKSAL (Afyonkarahisar) - You were not there.

ALİ MAHİR BAŞARIR (Mersin) - You didn't come, you should have come.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - Who asked for a quorum by roll call?

BURCU KÖKSAL (Afyonkarahisar) - You didn't come to the parliament.

ALİ MAHİR BAŞARIR (Mersin) - It was closed because you were not around.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - Why didn't you enter?

BURCU KÖKSAL (Afyonkarahisar) - You did not come to the parliament.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - Why didn't you enter after asking for a quorum by roll call?

ALİ MAHİR BAŞARIR (Mersin) - It was closed because you were not around.

BURCU KÖKSAL (Afyonkarahisar) - It was closed because your representatives did not come.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - You know, you want to work hard, but those who want a quorum by roll call do not take part in the roll call; Gee!

ALİ MAHİR BAŞARIR (Mersin) - It was closed because you were not there.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - The Parliament will work on Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday, but the Republican People's Party will not work due to its pleasure.

YUNUS EMRE (Istanbul) - Tell us about the raises!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Friends, despite you, we have implemented 310 legal regulations - including international agreements - regarding our beloved nation in the 27th Legislative Term, despite you. (Applause from AK PARTY ranks)

YUNUS EMRE (Istanbul) - Tell about the increases, the increases!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - We implemented it together with the People's Alliance, shoulder to shoulder, despite you; I want this to be known. (Noise from CHP benches)

Second: Article 5 of the Parliament's Internal Regulations states, "As of July 1, the Assembly will go into recess." says.

MAHİR POLAT (İzmir) - What are the 82 percent raises?

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Well, as of that date, the AK Party Group made a motion so that the Parliament would continue to work, right? You wanted to work, right? What did you say to our group proposal, sir? What did you say: "No, we do not want to work." You rejected it, didn't you? (Applause from the AK PARTY ranks) Oh my God!

ÖZGÜR ÖZEL (Manisa) - Come on!

ALİ MAHİR BAŞARIR (Mersin) - Lie, lie!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - Oh, wait a minute. AK PARTY Group says: "Let's continue working after July 1."

GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Come on, let's continue.

ÖZGÜR ÖZEL (Manisa) - Come on, come on!

ALİ MAHİR BAŞARIR (Mersin) - Okay, come on then; Come on, come on then.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continued) - Republican People's Party says: "No." Oh my God, I wonder why; I wonder why, I'm very curious!

GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Let's continue.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - We say continue because there is a raise; We tell the poor to continue because they are oppressed by the price increase.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - The day after the elections end, election activities begin in the AK PARTY.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - "Let's work" for the raise. We say, for a raise, raise.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continued) - We are not on holiday, we are in the election district. Our friends are currently working in the electoral districts, listening to our beloved nation and taking their notes. Hopefully, in October, we will implement the legal regulation that will serve our beloved nation and meet all their expectations; I would like these to be known too.

ÖZGÜR KARABAT (Çanakkale) - Leave the election, work for a living.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - The President announced in your new package, a new burden has arrived. What are you aware of?

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Another issue, dear friends, is an extraordinary meeting within the scope of the 7th article of our Parliament's Internal Regulations - Mr. Müsavat also mentioned it. Although, there is no concept of "extraordinary meeting" in our Internal Regulations, but there is a regulation regarding extraordinary assembly.

Well, there, along with its justification in the 7th article, it says that the extraordinary meeting will be held upon the request of at least 1 fifth of the deputies.

GÖKAN ZEYBEK (Istanbul) - What could be a bigger reason than raises?

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - So, how many times has our Parliament held extraordinary meetings so far, that is, after the 82 Constitution?

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - There has never been a raise like this before!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Including today's meeting, 52 meetings have been held. 6 of these...

HASAN UFUK ÇAKIR (Mersin) - Come to diesel, diesel! Come to diesel, Sir, come to diesel! Come raise, raise raise! (Noise from AK PARTY benches)

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - I will come.

HASAN UFUK ÇAKIR (Mersin) - Come on!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - I will come, I will come. I'll be back in a bit. First, let's express the issues raised by Mr. Özgür, and then we will come back; in order. That is not for you, Sir, Mr. Member of Parliament; in order; Ok. (Applause from AK PARTY ranks)

Well, according to the current situation, 120 MPs have requested this, with justification within the scope of the Internal Regulations, and our Parliament Presidency has decided to hold an extraordinary meeting as of today. Well, while the extraordinary meeting was decided, 6 of today's 52 demands were left unanswered or rejected, and different answers were given to the others. Do you know what was said there when looking at the reasons? There is no urgent situation that would necessitate holding an extraordinary meeting and opening a general discussion. Our legal friends know the concept of "extraordinary"; it means that a temporary and urgent situation has arisen. We passed a legal regulation that we voted on ten days ago, on July 15, around 01:00 at night. Along with the additional budget, an omnibus bill consisting of approximately 32 articles was passed along with the executive and enforcement. We discussed all the issues that are currently the justification for this law, namely the increases in SCT and VAT. So, what is our meeting phase? In total, the Assembly worked for eight hours at the General Assembly stage - related to the central budget and the tables in the management budget - eleven hours and twenty minutes at the Commission stage and seventeen hours and thirty-five minutes at the Commission stage. Here, the orators of all opposition parties have put forward all their thoughts on these issues, all of which have been evaluated.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - What percentage increase occurred after that law?

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Well, on this issue, which has been fully evaluated, what emergency, what duration of work came to the fore, and what created the need for this extraordinary meeting, and what has been said in response to this need?

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - What percentage increase, what percentage? All electricity and water supply in Şanlıurfa is cut off; How could it be any more? (Cries of "Listen, listen!" from the AK PARTY rows)

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - Friends, let's listen, please. Let me give you some answers first.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - I understand, Sir, I am telling you the extraordinary developments. What percentage increase was received? (Noise from AK PARTY benches)

CHAIRMAN - Wait a minute... Wait a minute...

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - Let's calm down, friends.

CHAIRMAN - Mr. Abdullah, wait a minute...

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - All the electricity in Şanlıurfa is out right now, in the temperature of 45 degrees, the electricity in Urfa is out, brother.

CHAIRMAN - Yes, Mr. Tanal...

Dear MPs, just now, while Mr. Özgür was speaking, a friend of ours from this group was making remarks, I sent a message and told him that it was not right to make remarks, but he did not speak again.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - President...

CHAIRMAN - Wait a minute... Tanal, you always talk, but brother!

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Look, was the Parliament closed on the 14th? Closed. Wasn't there a raise with the Presidential decree on the 15th? What more? What an extraordinary...

CHAIRMAN - They are calling you, a delegation has come from Urfa.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - I understand, the Parliament closed on the 14th, and the raise came on the 15th.

CHAIRMAN - Tanal, can you shut up a bit? But you always talk!

Here you go.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Dear friends, it is obvious that both the invitation and the request for an extraordinary meeting are needed in timed, calendared and urgent uses, in light of the past practices of our Assembly. Please, I draw your attention to this issue. Because, on August 5 last year, there was also an invitation by the MPs of the Republican People's Party, and the Parliament could not meet that day because there was no quorum. What was the reason? It was because he could not provide sufficient, convincing justification regarding its content, other political parties, urgency, time-limited, daily and calendared work.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - So, did we disturb your comfort?

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - Please let us know our sensitivity.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - I understand, so did we disturb your comfort? We called you for the sake of the nation, because the price increase is too high.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Our Assembly has made 310 legal regulations in the 27th Legislative Term, worked for more than ten thousand hours, and kept minutes of 300 thousand pages, which means that there is a hard-working Parliament that is in control of its work and agenda. Please do not accuse our friends as if they were on holiday.

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Oh, brother, the Parliament has not yet found a room for the citizens to sit in! They don't have a place to sit yet, what the hell is in charge! What does the parliament control? The MPs have no place to sit yet!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Although when you say "holiday"... "He is the person who knows everything on his own." We have a proverb: So our friends themselves may be on holiday, but none of our MPs...

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - Deputies don't have a room to sit yet, what do they control? They have no room to sit!

İBRAHİM EYYÜPOĞLU (Şanlıurfa) - Oh, know how to listen!

ÖZLEM ZENGİN (Istanbul) - Please, shut up!

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - They don't have a room to sit! How many deputies are idle!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - Friends... Friends...

MR BAYAR ÖZSOY (Kayseri) - What a shame!

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - What you have done is shameful, citizen, poor!

MR. BAYAR ÖZSOY (Kayseri) - What you did is shameful!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - Dear friends... Dear friends...

MAHMUT TANAL (Şanlıurfa) - You bring a raise, raise! What shame? Did we steal? Those who steal are doing shame!

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Dear friends, when we say "holiday", there is something in our nation's memory. For example, how can you go on vacation during an earthquake? For example, there has been a fire and flood, how can one go on vacation? For example, snow blocked the roads, right? Urgent public service is needed; How to go on holiday? (Applause from the ranks of the AK Party) For example, our nation knows the answer to this very well, but I know very well that all our MPs, including the People's Alliance, are not on holiday right now, they are on the field; Social media accounts are open and can be followed from the source. (Applause from the ranks of the AK PARTY) However, it is also clear who is on holiday and who is traveling on yachts and flats. Oh, I'm also telling you this, friends: A member of parliament is not a robot, and vacation is a very human right. So Mr. Özgür said in closing: "Two weeks, three weeks, four weeks... Everyone has a family, they have children..."

ÖZGÜR ÖZEL (Manisa) - A week or two... I say "One or two." I said, he is reasonable.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - Now "One or two." see records "Two or three weeks." You say, "Four weeks." you say; It's not important.

Friends, look, everyone has children, they have passed the exams, this is a very human need; I also find it shameful that you describe a need included in the law as if it were a special situation. Please correct these statements. Our MPs are also in the election district, not only here, they do not participate in the General Assembly activities, they do not just participate in the commissions, they also participate in the election districts outside the General Assembly, especially all the MPs of the 11 provinces that experienced the earthquake are there every day. Because we had a very heavy earthquake, we experienced a major disaster whose total cost to our country will reach 104 billion dollars in the coming period. We experienced a disaster that would have devastated a country the size of Bulgaria, just as big. From infrastructure to superstructure, providing employment there, establishing industrial zones again, I thank all the MPs. Within the omnibus law we passed last time, we implemented many regulations regarding both AFAD-related rights ownership and the establishment of new industrial zones by our Ministry of Industry and Technology; Our friends are currently following them, and I thank them. Therefore, we do not have a "holiday" problem, we are always ready to work in the service of our beloved nation.

Secondly, Mr. Özgür talked about some journalists in prison. The independent judiciary continues the trials in its impartial and independent manner.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Journalists were detained today, today, today.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Okay, but when we think about the members of the press in prison, I wonder why we don't think about this: I wonder if you will have a statement regarding the journalists and members of the press who are paid by these municipalities and parties. A sad document was published, wasn't it? Some journalists were documented; A sad document was published, about how many million TL per year, an agreement was made with I don't know what kind of television. I guess an answer could have been given to this on July 24, Journalists and Press Day, right? I think they will respond to the idea that members of the press are in prison.

MERAL DANIŞ BEŞTAŞ (Erzurum) - Today, journalists were detained today.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Dear friends, AK PARTY's twenty-year...

GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (Istanbul) - You are talking about a protocol that has been audited by the Constitutional Court and the Court of Accounts.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - I wish you had disclosed this protocol clearly while making it.

GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (Istanbul) - A protocol audited by the Constitutional Court and the Court of Accounts...

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - Not with a notice from the notary, but while everyone else is doing it...

GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (Istanbul) - We are not talking about partisan media.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continuing) - Well, we would be pleased if you could explain who gets paid from these municipalities and these things.

GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Let's explain it all, ask, let's explain it all.

ABDULLAH GÜLER (Continue) - Dear friends, we have achieved many gains during the twenty-year AK PARTY rule. We, as the AK PARTY, aim to grow at an average annual rate of 5.5 percent between 2024 and 2028, to reach a national income of 1.5 trillion dollars by the end of 2028, to reach a per capita income level of 16 thousand dollars, to increase the total employment, which is around 32 million by the end of 2022, by 6 million to 38 million by the end of 2028, to reduce the unemployment rate below 7 percent by ensuring that the unemployment rate gradually declines, We aim to increase our exports, which are 254.2 billion dollars by the end of 2022, to at least 400 billion dollars in 2028, to increase the number of tourists visiting our country in the new period to 90 million and the total income to 100 billion dollars, to reduce inflation to single digit figures, and to increase TL savings by further developing financial instruments. We are also determined to ensure stronger coordination in monetary, fiscal, income and financial sector policies. We will continue to maintain and strengthen fiscal discipline as an anchor that increases confidence in our economy.

Mr. President, dear MPs; The common goal of economic policies is to achieve balance in income distribution, price stability and full employment. We have effective tools that can also be used in financial policies to successfully carry out targeted policies. The most important of these is tax. Tax is an effective fiscal policy tool in many areas, from income distribution to determining the prices of goods and services. For this reason, taxes in many countries vary depending on country conditions. As we have just mentioned, the earthquake, which was considered as the disaster of the century and cost 104 billion dollars, caused a serious burden on our country's economy and public finance. For this reason, it became necessary to make tax regulations in order to compensate for some of the financial effects of the earthquake. Our revenue measures taken to reduce the additional costs caused by the earthquake and to re-establish budget discipline will also contribute to strengthening the coordination between monetary policy and fiscal policy in the fight against inflation.

Mr. President, dear MPs; The important variable used to analyze the tax structures of countries is the concept of tax burden, which shows the share of total tax revenue in gross domestic product. The composition of tax revenues and the sectoral change in growth, together with the change in this rate over time, contain important information about the tax structure and growth. I would like to point out here that Türkiye is one of the countries with the lowest tax burden among OECD countries. The proportional weight of indirect and direct taxes in this composition varies according to political preference and the needs of the country. As AK PARTY, we will determine and continue to determine the tax burden and composition in line with the interests of our nation and our economic goals, without compromising fiscal discipline in the coming period. In addition to monetary policy, we aim to grow in stability and confidence by reinforcing financial stability in Türkiye with fiscal policy and structural reforms. In line with our Türkiye Century vision, we will increase employment and continue to distribute the blessings of growth fairly, as always. We will continue to pursue our well-defined and result-oriented reform steps in line with our national values ​​in order to improve the investment environment in Türkiye. In the medium-term program, we will update public policies with macro indicators, introduce structural reforms and direct resource allocation within this framework. We would like to underline once again that, under the leadership of our President, Mr. Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, we continue our struggle to meet our country's goals without slowing down and we will continue to do so.
records.englishTitle: On the occasion of the preliminary discussions of the proposal (8/12) to open a general discussion in order to find solutions to the problems arising from the increase in VAT and SCT rates and the increase in prices of basic needs products.

englishFields.Subject: On the occasion of the preliminary discussions of the proposal (8/12) to open a general discussion in order to find solutions to the problems arising from the increase in VAT and SCT rates and the increase in prices of basic needs products.

records.title: Anayasa Mahkemesinin Şerafettin Can Atalay'ın milletvekilliğinin düşmesi işlemiyle ilgili vermiş olduğu kararı doğrultusunda TBMM'nin tutum ve davranışının belirlenmesi, yaşanan bu durumun sebeplerinin tespit edilmesi ve benzer durumların önlenmesi konusunda bir genel görüşme açılmasına ilişkin önergenin (8/43) ön görüşmesi münasebetiyle
